provider.ts.purpose: Provider Service, model catalog, SDK resolution, default model selection

transform.ts.purpose: Message transformation, temperature/topP/topK normalization, caching, reasoning conversion

auth.ts.purpose: Provider OAuth authorization flow via plugin hooks

bundled_sdks.description: 23 SDK packages lazy-loaded via dynamic imports, keyed by npm package name. Each provides a factory that returns { languageModel(id) }.

custom_providers.description: Custom loader function `custom(dep: CustomDep)` registers provider-specific initialization logic. Each custom loader receives the provider Info and returns autoload flag + optional model loader, vars loader, options, and model discovery.

model_shape.api: { npm: string, id: string } (SDK package + model ID)
model_shape.name: string

provider_info_shape.name: string
provider_info_shape.env: string[] (env var names for API keys)

provider_configuration.allow_list: enabled_providers (Set<string>) — if set, only these are allowed
provider_configuration.api_keys: Resolved from env vars (provider.env list) or plugin auth hooks

authentication.mechanism: AWS credential chain (env, config, profile, SSO, IMDS)

authentication.for: github-copilot, gitlab, openai-codex, azure, cloudflare, digitalocean, xai, poe

streaming.architecture: AI SDK v3 LanguageModelV3 interface handles streaming natively
streaming.ssr_wrapper: wrapSSE(response, ms, ctl) — wraps Response body into ReadableStream with SSE timeout

timeouts.header_timeout: Aborts if response headers don't arrive within ms
timeouts.chunk_timeout: Aborts if no SSE chunk within ms (per AbortController)

sdk_routing.useLanguageModel: function to detect languageModel-only SDKs (no chat/responses)
sdk_routing.copilot_responses: shouldUseCopilotResponsesApi() — uses sdk.responses() for gpt-* and o* models in github-copilot

streaming.transform_pipeline: ProviderTransform.message() normalizes messages per provider before streaming

code_smells_and_risks.description: SDK resolution, model catalog loading, custom loaders, and the Service implementation all live in one file. Hard to test in isolation, high coupling.
useLanguageModel(sdk: any) and CustomModelLoader(sdk: any) use `any` for SDK objects, losing type safety at the provider SDK boundary.
const oldest = order.shift()! uses `!` — if order is empty, this crashes at runtime.
const priority = ['gpt-5', 'claude-sonnet-4', 'big-pickle', 'gemini-3-pro'] — hardcoded priority string list, not configurable.
export * as Provider from './provider' — self-referencing re-export at the end of the file.

index.ts.purpose: Plugin Service, internal plugins, hook execution, auth plugin integration

loader.ts.purpose: Plugin discovery, resolution, loading from npm/local/url sources

cloudflare.ts.purpose: Cloudflare AI Gateway + Workers auth plugins

github-copilot/.purpose: GitHub Copilot auth plugin (copilot.ts, device-code.ts, oauth.ts)

plugin_lifecycle.discovery: PluginLoader.loadExternal() discovers plugins from config sources (npm packages, local paths, URLs)
plugin_lifecycle.resolution: PluginLoader.resolve() resolves plugin identifiers to packages and loads package.json
plugin_lifecycle.loading: PluginLoader.load() loads the plugin module and extracts hooks
plugin_lifecycle.application: applyPlugin() registers hooks, tools, and auth handlers from loaded plugins
plugin_lifecycle.health: PluginHealthStore tracks plugin crash/error counts with quarantine after threshold
plugin_lifecycle.quarantine: Plugins are automatically quarantined after repeated crashes, can be unquarantined manually
plugin_lifecycle.cleanup: No explicit dispose — plugins live for the session lifetime

plugin_types.internal: Built-in plugins for auth providers (copilot, codex, gitlab, poe, cloudflare, azure, digitalocean, xai)

hooks_interface.auth: Authenticate with providers (OAuth, device code, API key)
hooks_interface.tool.register: Register custom tools (checked via capability system)

plugin_registry.state: { plugins: PluginRegistration[], hooks: Hooks[], health: PluginHealthStore }
methods: trigger(name, event) → Effect<void> (invoke all hooks for an event)

capability_system.description: Plugin capabilities are checked via checkCapability(pluginId, 'tool.register'). HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP maps hook names to required capabilities.
capability_system.example: tool.register → requires capability check before external plugins can register tools

auth_plugin_integration.description: Auth plugins (CopilotAuthPlugin, CodexAuthPlugin, GitlabAuthPlugin, etc.) are initialized during Plugin.init() and registered as Auth providers. The Provider.auth service resolves API keys and OAuth tokens via these plugins.
auth_plugin_integration.flow: Plugin.init() → internalPlugins() → register auth hooks → Provider.auth queries hooks → returns API key / token

experimental_features.websocket: experimentalWebSocketsEnabled() — enables WebSocket bridge for plugin communication

code_smells_and_risks.description: applyPlugin() loads and executes external plugin code in the same process with full access. No isolation, no resource limits.
HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP is a string-keyed record. No typed capabilities enum. Tool registration checks capability but there's no authorization for syscalls or network access.
Plugin loading doesn't verify version compatibility between the plugin and the host. Missing semver range check.

code_smells_and_risks.issue: Health store is filesystem-based but not validated on read
code_smells_and_risks.description: loadPluginHealthStore reads from disk without schema validation.

tool.ts.purpose: Core Tool.Def interface, execution context, error classes, metadata types

registry.ts.purpose: ToolRegistry Service — built-in tool registration, plugin tool integration, schema conversion

execution.ts.purpose: Tool execution event publishing (started, completed, failed)

typed-result.ts.purpose: Typed result helper for tool outputs

tool_registry_service.plugin_integration: fromPlugin(id, def) converts @opencode-ai/plugin ToolDefinition to Tool.Def. Plugin tools prefixed with 'plugin_' when colliding with built-in IDs.

flow: 2. resolve() loads ToolRegistry, Plugin, MCP services
3. Tool definitions merged from: built-in + plugin tools + MCP tools
4. Each tool converted to AI SDK tool format (tool() from 'ai')
6. Tool executed via execute() wrapper with semaphore and timeout
tool_execution_pipeline.semaphore: toolSemaphore (default concurrency limit per tool type)
tool_execution_pipeline.schema_conversion: Zod → JSONSchema7 conversion for plugin tools using zod.toJSONSchema()

session_tool_resolution.description: resolve() function combines tool definitions from ToolRegistry, Plugin tools, and MCP tools into AI SDK-compatible tool objects. Handles parameter schema conversion, execution wrapping, and permission integration.

code_smells_and_risks.description: BUILTIN_TOOL_IDS has 80+ entries. Many tools overlap in functionality (smart_bash vs shell, smart_grep vs grep). Tool sprawl makes security auditing and testing difficult.

code_smells_and_risks.issue: Plugin tool naming collision protection is weak
code_smells_and_risks.description: Plugin tools with built-in names get 'plugin_' prefix, but no dedup for multiple plugins with the same name.

code_smells_and_risks.issue: Schema conversion: Zod → JSONSchema7 is lossy
code_smells_and_risks.description: zodJsonSchema() uses zod.toJSONSchema() which can lose metadata and validation refinements.

code_smells_and_risks.issue: No tool execution sandboxing for plugin tools
code_smells_and_risks.description: Plugin tools execute in the same Effect runtime as built-in tools with no resource isolation.

index.ts.purpose: MCP Service — server connection, tool discovery, tool conversion, OAuth flow

auth.ts.purpose: MCP OAuth token storage and management

transport_support.oauth_support: StreamableHTTPClientTransport and SSEClientTransport support OAuth via authProvider. Stdio does not.

flow: 1. MCP Service connects to configured servers on startup
2. listTools() discovers tools from each server
3. convertMcpTool() converts MCP Tool → AI SDK Tool format
4. SessionTools.resolve() merges MCP tools with built-in and plugin tools
6. Tool execution calls mcpClient.callTool() via the appropriate transport
7. MCP tools auto-disconnect on server crash with health watching
mcp_tool_integration.tool_conversion: convertMcpTool() wraps MCP tool schema in dynamicTool() from AI SDK. Handles outputSchema validation with error detection.
mcp_tool_integration.health_watching: Each MCP server is watched for tool list changes (ToolListChangedNotificationSchema). Servers are reconnected on disconnect.

code_smells_and_risks.issue: No MCP tool tool_use_id mapping verification
code_smells_and_risks.description: MCP tool execution doesn't verify that tool responses correspond to the correct request IDs. Could lead to response confusion if multiple concurrent calls.

code_smells_and_risks.issue: Stdio process management — PID tracking but no cleanup on abnormal exit
code_smells_and_risks.description: const pid = client.transport.pid tracks the PID but there's no explicit child process cleanup. Relies on GC.
Token storage doesn't mention encryption. If tokens are stored on disk, they should be encrypted.
No rate limiting on MCP tool calls. A misbehaving server or prompt could flood the MCP server.

5_cross_cutting_observations.architecture_pattern: Effect v4 service layers with Context.Tag-style dependency injection. All major subsystems are Effect Services with Layer-based composition.
5_cross_cutting_observations.layer_composition: Services compose via Layer.provide() chain. Bootstrap runtime wires all layers.
common_weaknesses: No resource sandboxing for external plugin or MCP tools
Large monolithic files (provider.ts 1916 lines, mcp/index.ts 1053 lines, registry.ts 866 lines)
Type safety gaps at SDK boundaries (any types in provider SDK resolution)
No encryption for stored OAuth tokens
Missing version compatibility checks for plugins

security_posture.auth: Multi-provider OAuth + API key support via plugin hooks. Google Cloud and AWS credential chains supported.
security_posture.tools: Permission.Service provides action-based tool authorization (allow/ask/deny). Per-session permission rules.
security_posture.mcp: OAuth for streamable HTTP and SSE transports. Stdio transport has no auth.
security_posture.plugins: Capability-based checks for tool registration. No runtime sandboxing for external plugins.
security_posture.environment: API keys sourced from env vars, config, or auth plugins. No key logging detected.
